families.query_basis: Reviewed the current official TennCare application page for live Medicaid application and local help routes.
families.status_reason: Reviewed 2026-06-25 the live official TennCare application page. It states there are three ways to apply for TennCare Medicaid and Medicare Savings Programs, preserves TennCare Connect online and phone application routes, and preserves local AAAD and DDA help routes for applicants who need disability-related application support.

samples.sample_name: How Do I Apply for TennCare?
samples.evidence_snippet: There are three ways you can apply for TennCare Medicaid and Medicare Savings Programs (MSP). You can apply online, over the phone, or with a paper application.

must_include: There are three ways you can apply for TennCare Medicaid and Medicare Savings Programs (MSP).
TennCare Connect
apply for coverage, check your status, report changes, read letters, and more
If you have an intellectual disability
Department of Disability and Aging (DDA)

families.query_basis: Reviewed current official TennCare HCBS waiver and ECF CHOICES pages for live I/DD waiver evidence.
families.status_reason: Reviewed 2026-06-25 the live official TennCare 1915(c) HCBS waivers page and Employment and Community First CHOICES page. Together they preserve Tennessee's HCBS waiver structure for people with intellectual disabilities and the current I/DD-focused ECF CHOICES program with live application and DDA regional-office help routes.

samples.evidence_snippet: Tennessee's 1915(c) waiver programs provide Home and Community Based Services (HCBS) for people with intellectual disabilities as an alternative to institutional care.
Employment and Community First CHOICES is for people of all ages who have an intellectual or developmental disability (I/DD).

must_include: Tennessee's 1915(c) waiver programs provide Home and Community Based Services (HCBS) for people with intellectual disabilities
Tennessee has three 1915(c) waivers
Employment and Community First CHOICES is for people of all ages who have an intellectual or developmental disability (I/DD).
If you do not have TennCare you can get help by calling your Department of Disability and Aging (DDA) Regional Office for free.

families.query_basis: Reviewed the current official DDA intake page for live I/DD authority and regional intake routing.
families.status_reason: Reviewed 2026-06-25 the live official Department of Disability and Aging intake page. It explicitly covers intake for people with intellectual and developmental disabilities and directs applicants to the regional office in their area with separate West, Middle, and East Tennessee contact numbers.

samples.evidence_snippet: The Department of Disability and Aging conducts intake activities for various programs for people with intellectual and developmental disabilities.

must_include: The Department of Disability and Aging conducts intake activities for various programs for people with intellectual and developmental disabilities

families.query_basis: Reviewed the current official TEIS referral page and TEIS district-office dataset for live local Part C routing.
families.status_reason: Reviewed 2026-06-25 the live official Tennessee Early Intervention System page and TEIS district-office dataset. The referral workflow asks for the child's county of residence and the official district-office dataset preserves district names, addresses, cities, and phone numbers for local TEIS offices.

samples.evidence_snippet: You may make a referral to TEIS by completing our online referral form or by calling (800) 852-7157. Please be prepared to provide the child's county of residence.
The district-office dataset lists TEIS district offices including First in Johnson City, East in Knoxville, Greater Nashville in Nashville, and Memphis Delta in Memphis.

families.query_basis: Reviewed the current official Tennessee special-education page for IDEA Part B evidence after the inherited URL returned 404.
families.status_reason: Reviewed 2026-06-25 the live official Tennessee Department of Education special-education page on its current family-support path. It preserves statewide special-education program language, IDEA Part B guidance, and district obligations to provide high-quality education to students with disabilities.

samples.evidence_snippet: The department promotes educational services and programs for all Tennessee's students with special education needs.

must_include: The department promotes educational services and programs for all Tennessee's students with special education needs.
The Individuals with Disabilities Education Act (IDEA) is a federal law ensuring appropriate services to children with disabilities throughout the nation.
the commitment made by districts and schools to provide a high-quality education in an inclusive setting to all students with disabilities is crucial to their success

families.query_basis: Reviewed the current official Tennessee School Directory page and its built-in district schema fields.
